_meta.source: WHO Model List of Essential Medicines (23rd List) & FDA Labels
_meta.dataset_scope: Phase 1: High-Frequency Essential Medicines (Common OTC, Chronic, Acute)
_meta.disclaimer: CRITICAL: This database is for informational AI retrieval only. It is NOT a substitute for a doctor's prescription or advice.

medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.uses: Mild to moderate pain (headache, muscle ache); fever reduction.

dosage.child: 10–15 mg/kg every 4–6 hours (do not exceed 5 doses in 24 hours).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Swallow with water. Can be taken on empty stomach. Liquid forms must be measured with a dosing syringe.
medicines.side_effects: Rare. Serious liver damage can occur with overdose or if taken with alcohol.
medicines.warnings: Do not exceed the recommended dose. Check other medicines (cold/flu) for paracetamol content to avoid accidental overdose.
medicines.pregnancy: Category B. Generally considered safe if used as directed.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.uses: Pain relief, fever reduction, inflammation (arthritis, injury, menstrual cramps).

dosage.adult: 200–400 mg every 4–6 hours (max 3200 mg/day under supervision, 1200 mg OTC).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take with food or milk to prevent stomach upset.
medicines.side_effects: Stomach pain, heartburn, dizziness, risk of bleeding, kidney stress.
medicines.warnings: Avoid if you have a history of stomach ulcers, GI bleeding, or kidney problems. May increase heart attack risk.
medicines.pregnancy: Category C (Avoid in 3rd trimester - Category D).

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.uses: Bacterial infections including pneumonia, dental abscesses, ear infections, and UTIs.

dosage.adult: 250–500 mg every 8 hours or 875 mg every 12 hours.

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Can be taken with or without food. Complete the full course even if feeling better.
medicines.warnings: Do not use if allergic to penicillin. Immediate medical attention needed for signs of anaphylaxis (swelling, trouble breathing).

medicines.name: Metformin
medicines.uses: First-line treatment for Type 2 diabetes mellitus to control blood sugar.

dosage.adult: Initial: 500 mg twice daily with meals. Maintenance: up to 2000–2550 mg daily.
dosage.child: Consult specialist (Approved for 10+ years in some regions).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take with meals to reduce stomach upset/nausea.
medicines.warnings: Risk of lactic acidosis (rare but serious). Stop temporarily before iodinated contrast scans.

medicines.name: Lisinopril
medicines.uses: Hypertension (high blood pressure), heart failure, post-heart attack recovery.

dosage.adult: Initial: 10 mg once daily. Maintenance: 20–40 mg daily.
dosage.child: Not typically recommended without specialist advice.

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take at the same time each day.
medicines.side_effects: Dry persistent cough, dizziness, headache, fatigue, hyperkalemia (high potassium).
medicines.warnings: Discontinue as soon as pregnancy is detected (Boxed Warning). Can cause angioedema.

medicines.name: Atorvastatin
medicines.uses: Lowers high cholesterol (LDL) and triglycerides; reduces risk of stroke/heart attack.

dosage.child: 10 mg daily (only for specific genetic lipid conditions).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take at any time of day, with or without food. Consistent timing preferred.
medicines.side_effects: Muscle pain, weakness, liver enzyme changes, digestive upset.
medicines.warnings: Avoid large amounts of grapefruit juice. Monitor for unexplained muscle pain/weakness (rhabdomyolysis).

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.uses: Hay fever, seasonal allergy symptoms, hives (urticaria).

dosage.child: 2.5–5 mg once daily depending on age/weight.

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Swallow with water. One tablet a day.
medicines.side_effects: Drowsiness (less than 1st gen), dry mouth, fatigue.
medicines.warnings: Use caution when driving or operating machinery until you know how it affects you.

medicines.name: Azithromycin
medicines.uses: Respiratory infections (bronchitis), ear infections, skin infections, STIs.

dosage.adult: 500 mg day 1, then 250 mg days 2–5 (Z-Pak regimen).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take with or without food. Do not take aluminum/magnesium antacids simultaneously.
medicines.warnings: Can cause heart rhythm changes (QT prolongation). Caution in liver disease.

medicines.name: Omeprazole
medicines.uses: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), acid reflux, stomach ulcers.

dosage.adult: 20 mg once daily before a meal (usually breakfast).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take on an empty stomach, at least 30-60 mins before breakfast.
medicines.warnings: Long-term use (>1 year) may weaken bones or cause B12/Magnesium deficiency.
medicines.pregnancy: Category C. Use only if clearly needed.

medicines.name: Amlodipine
medicines.uses: Hypertension (high blood pressure), chronic stable angina.
medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take at the same time every day.
medicines.side_effects: Swelling of ankles/feet (edema), flushing, palpitations, dizziness.
medicines.warnings: Tell doctor if you have liver disease. Do not stop abruptly for angina.

medicines.name: Levothyroxine

dosage.adult: Usually 25–200 mcg daily (highly individualized based on TSH).

medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take in the morning on an empty stomach, 30-60 mins before breakfast. Separate from calcium/iron by 4 hours.
medicines.side_effects: Hair loss (temporary), appetite change. Overdose causes hyperthyroid symptoms (racing heart, shaking).
medicines.warnings: Do not use for weight loss. Narrow therapeutic index requires regular blood tests.
medicines.pregnancy: Category A. Safe and often necessary to increase dose during pregnancy.

medicines.name: Albuterol
medicines.uses: Asthma attacks, bronchospasm, COPD, exercise-induced asthma.

dosage.child: 1–2 puffs as needed (use spacer if young).

medicines.how_to_take: Inhalation: Shake well. Exhale, seal lips, inhale while puffing. Hold breath 10s.
medicines.side_effects: Tremors (shaking hands), nervousness, rapid heart rate.
medicines.warnings: Seek emergency help if inhaler does not relieve breathing difficulty. Do not exceed frequency.

medicines.name: Sertraline
medicines.uses: Depression, panic disorder, OCD, PTSD, social anxiety.
medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take at same time daily (morning or evening).
medicines.warnings: Black Box Warning: Increased risk of suicidal thinking in young adults during initiation. Do not stop abruptly.

medicines.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Take in the morning to avoid night-time urination.
medicines.side_effects: Frequent urination, electrolyte imbalance (low potassium/sodium), dizziness.

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.how_to_take: Oral: Space doses evenly (no more than 12h gap).
medicines.warnings: Risk of respiratory depression if combined with opioids. Suicidal thoughts (rare).

warnings: Do not use for viral infections
Some variants banned in many countries
